data: Question 1
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
What is gravity?

[{'answer_text': 'The force that pulls objects toward the center of the planet.', 'text': 'The force that pulls objects toward the center of the planet.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'The force that pushes objects up into the sky.', 'text': 'The force that pushes objects up into the sky.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The force that makes things spin in circles.', 'text': 'The force that makes things spin in circles.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The energy used to build a machine.', 'text': 'The energy used to build a machine.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The speed of wind hitting an object.', 'text': 'The speed of wind hitting an object.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The material used to make a parachute.', 'text': 'The material used to make a parachute.', 'weight': 0}]
The force that pulls objects toward the center of the planet.
The force that pushes objects up into the sky.
The force that makes things spin in circles.
The energy used to build a machine.
The speed of wind hitting an object.
The material used to make a parachute.
Question 2
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
What is air resistance?

[{'answer_text': 'The heat caused by two objects rubbing together.', 'text': 'The heat caused by two objects rubbing together.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'A force that pushes upward against a falling object.', 'text': 'A force that pushes upward against a falling object.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'A type of heavy material used for building.', 'text': 'A type of heavy material used for building.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The invisible pull of a magnet on metal.', 'text': 'The invisible pull of a magnet on metal.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The time it takes for an object to hit the ground.', 'text': 'The time it takes for an object to hit the ground.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The mathematical average of an experiment.', 'text': 'The mathematical average of an experiment.', 'weight': 0}]
The heat caused by two objects rubbing together.
A force that pushes upward against a falling object.
A type of heavy material used for building.
The invisible pull of a magnet on metal.
The time it takes for an object to hit the ground.
The mathematical average of an experiment.
Question 3
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
Why does a wide, flat object fall more slowly than a thin, narrow object?

[{'answer_text': 'It is much heavier than the thin object.', 'text': 'It is much heavier than the thin object.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'It has less gravity acting on it.', 'text': 'It has less gravity acting on it.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'It has to push more air out of the way, creating more air resistance.', 'text': 'It has to push more air out of the way, creating more air resistance.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'It uses electricity to float in the air.', 'text': 'It uses electricity to float in the air.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The thin object has a parachute attached to it.', 'text': 'The thin object has a parachute attached to it.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The wide object cuts through the air like a knife.', 'text': 'The wide object cuts through the air like a knife.', 'weight': 0}]
It is much heavier than the thin object.
It has less gravity acting on it.
It has to push more air out of the way, creating more air resistance.
It uses electricity to float in the air.
The thin object has a parachute attached to it.
The wide object cuts through the air like a knife.
Question 4
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
How do objects like airplanes and diving birds move quickly through the air?

[{'answer_text': 'They have large, wide parachutes.', 'text': 'They have large, wide parachutes.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'They are very light and float easily.', 'text': 'They are very light and float easily.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'They ignore the rules of gravity entirely.', 'text': 'They ignore the rules of gravity entirely.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'They have narrow, slanted shapes that cut through the air.', 'text': 'They have narrow, slanted shapes that cut through the air.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'They have a very large surface area.', 'text': 'They have a very large surface area.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'They always travel in large groups.', 'text': 'They always travel in large groups.', 'weight': 0}]
They have large, wide parachutes.
They are very light and float easily.
They ignore the rules of gravity entirely.
They have narrow, slanted shapes that cut through the air.
They have a very large surface area.
They always travel in large groups.
Question 5
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
What is the main purpose of a parachute?

[{'answer_text': 'To make an object fall faster.', 'text': 'To make an object fall faster.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To make an object heavier.', 'text': 'To make an object heavier.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To cut through the air sharply.', 'text': 'To cut through the air sharply.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To protect people from rain and snow.', 'text': 'To protect people from rain and snow.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To capture air and safely slow down a falling object.', 'text': 'To capture air and safely slow down a falling object.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'To measure the distance from the sky to the ground.', 'text': 'To measure the distance from the sky to the ground.', 'weight': 0}]
To make an object fall faster.
To make an object heavier.
To cut through the air sharply.
To protect people from rain and snow.
To capture air and safely slow down a falling object.
To measure the distance from the sky to the ground.
Question 6
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
During a science experiment, why is it important to drop an object from the exact same height every time?

[{'answer_text': 'To make the object bounce higher.', 'text': 'To make the object bounce higher.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To change the force of gravity.', 'text': 'To change the force of gravity.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To make the object fall faster.', 'text': 'To make the object fall faster.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To practice measuring lengths.', 'text': 'To practice measuring lengths.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To give the parachute a different shape.', 'text': 'To give the parachute a different shape.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To keep the test fair and accurate.', 'text': 'To keep the test fair and accurate.', 'weight': 100}]
To make the object bounce higher.
To change the force of gravity.
To make the object fall faster.
To practice measuring lengths.
To give the parachute a different shape.
To keep the test fair and accurate.
Question 7
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
Why is it important to repeat a drop test 10 times instead of just once?

[{'answer_text': 'To find an accurate average and make sure the results are reliable.', 'text': 'To find an accurate average and make sure the results are reliable.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'To use up all the extra class time.', 'text': 'To use up all the extra class time.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To make the parachute break faster.', 'text': 'To make the parachute break faster.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To change the rules of the experiment halfway through.', 'text': 'To change the rules of the experiment halfway through.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To see if gravity stops working after the 5th drop.', 'text': 'To see if gravity stops working after the 5th drop.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To make the object heavier with each drop.', 'text': 'To make the object heavier with each drop.', 'weight': 0}]
To find an accurate average and make sure the results are reliable.
To use up all the extra class time.
To make the parachute break faster.
To change the rules of the experiment halfway through.
To see if gravity stops working after the 5th drop.
To make the object heavier with each drop.
Question 8
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
If you record the time it takes an object to fall for 10 trials, how do you find the "average" time?

[{'answer_text': 'You only look at the fastest time and ignore the rest.', 'text': 'You only look at the fastest time and ignore the rest.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'You add all the times together and divide by 10.', 'text': 'You add all the times together and divide by 10.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'You guess the number that looks the best.', 'text': 'You guess the number that looks the best.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'You subtract the first time from the last time.', 'text': 'You subtract the first time from the last time.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'You multiply the first time by 10.', 'text': 'You multiply the first time by 10.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'You pick the slowest time and double it.', 'text': 'You pick the slowest time and double it.', 'weight': 0}]
You only look at the fastest time and ignore the rest.
You add all the times together and divide by 10.
You guess the number that looks the best.
You subtract the first time from the last time.
You multiply the first time by 10.
You pick the slowest time and double it.
Question 9
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
Why do scientists measure the time difference between dropping a plain object and dropping it with a parachute?

[{'answer_text': 'To see which one makes a louder noise when it hits the ground.', 'text': 'To see which one makes a louder noise when it hits the ground.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To check if the plain object can float away.', 'text': 'To check if the plain object can float away.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To calculate exactly how much the parachute slowed down the fall.', 'text': 'To calculate exactly how much the parachute slowed down the fall.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'To see if the parachute makes the object fall faster.', 'text': 'To see if the parachute makes the object fall faster.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To practice using a stopwatch on different settings.', 'text': 'To practice using a stopwatch on different settings.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To prove that parachutes do not change anything.', 'text': 'To prove that parachutes do not change anything.', 'weight': 0}]
To see which one makes a louder noise when it hits the ground.
To check if the plain object can float away.
To calculate exactly how much the parachute slowed down the fall.
To see if the parachute makes the object fall faster.
To practice using a stopwatch on different settings.
To prove that parachutes do not change anything.

multiple_choice_question
In the engineering design process, what should you do first when given a new challenge?

[{'answer_text': 'Immediately test your ideas.', 'text': 'Immediately test your ideas.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': "Throw away materials you don't like.", 'text': "Throw away materials you don't like.", 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Evaluate the final product.', 'text': 'Evaluate the final product.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Think about the materials, brainstorm, and plan a design.', 'text': 'Think about the materials, brainstorm, and plan a design.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': "Copy someone else's finished work.", 'text': "Copy someone else's finished work.", 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Write down the final average time.', 'text': 'Write down the final average time.', 'weight': 0}]

Throw away materials you don't like.
Evaluate the final product.
Think about the materials, brainstorm, and plan a design.
Copy someone else's finished work.
Write down the final average time.
